steps.title: Trace the text’s structure
steps.content: Sentence 1 states historians agree the Piast dynasty was crucial to forming the Polish state. The word “But” in sentence 2 introduces a split within that agreement: some historians distinguish between well-documented rulers like Witold Bolesławowic and figures like Radomir whose historical existence is disputed because his record relies on oral tradition rather than documentary evidence. The text’s whole movement is from unified agreement $\Rightarrow$ disagreement about how certain members of the dynasty should be treated.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Test each option against what the text actually does:
  • Choice A — ‘It favorably compares the reign of Radomir with the reign of Witold Bolesławowic.’: the text does not rank or favor either reign; it contrasts how well-documented they are, not how good they were. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It describes a process that was used to authenticate a particular Polish medieval text.’: the Gesta principum Polonorum is mentioned as the source for Radomir, not as a text whose authenticity is being established by a described process. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘It presents evidence in support of a particular claim about Witold Bolesławowic.’: Witold is cited only as an example of a well-documented Piast ruler; the text makes no specific claim about him and marshals no evidence for such a claim. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘It explains why historians differ in their treatment of an aspect of Polish history.’: the “But” shift signals the disagreement, and the rest of the passage explains it — some Piast figures have documentary records while others rely on oral tradition, leading historians to treat them differently. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
